---
title: "Data"
output: html_document
---

# Datasets

Every economist is responsible for their own data. Here is mine.

---

## Underlying Data for Published Work on This Site

### *Duration and Public Finance in the United States, 2001–2023*
- [Source data and processing scripts](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1WYw1BDLebc86p4GOkAUdjdTOUOvrPhm5?usp=sharing)
- [Read the paper →](economichistory.html)

### Tax Revenue Cyclicality
- [Source data](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1LZOTT-_jnL9rwczvfLr8AFgGP3hQKDaV?usp=sharing)

---

## HMDA — Home Mortgage Disclosure Act

The HMDA Explore Shiny app at [andenick.shinyapps.io/HMDAExplore](https://andenick.shinyapps.io/HMDAExplore/) is the public-facing version of this work.

- [HMDA processing code (with data)](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1wX-A157XSy2lfMjoSgbpTU8s0PbH9Jyu?usp=sharing) — fair warning: this is not a perfect replication package; you will need to adjust file paths if you run the code, and the underlying files are large enough that a laptop may not have the RAM to process them in full.
- [HMDA Shiny app source](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1w_3igaXfA0My0gK4gJpa0jg_d1KSxqff?usp=sharing)

---

## Public Banking Data Infrastructure

For programmatic access to bank regulatory data spanning 1863 to the present:

- **FreeNIC** — [github.com/andenick/FreeNIC](https://github.com/andenick/FreeNIC) — 1.5 billion rows, 217,210 institutions, 20 source datasets, 15 MCP tools.

---

## Anwar Shaikh Archive

35 boxes / 70,000+ scanned pages at the Levy Economics Institute of Bard College. Public archive:

- [digitalcommons.bard.edu/as_archive](https://digitalcommons.bard.edu/as_archive/)
- Companion site: [realecon.org](https://realecon.org)

---

<style type="text/css">
.navbar-default {
    background-color: #640000 !important;
}
.navbar .navbar-brand, .navbar .navbar-nav .nav-links  {
  color: white;
}
.navbar-default .navbar-nav>li>a {
    color: white !important;
    font-weight: 150;
}
.navbar-nav > .active > a {
    background-color: #006400 !important;
    font-weight: 175;
}
</style>
